        pt.is_empty(),
        pt.l4_table@.dom().contains(pt.cr3),

        p != pt.cr3,
    ensures
        pt.page_closure().contains(p),
{
}

        0 <= l4i < pt.kernel_l4_end,

        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().perm.present == false,
{
}

name: resolve_1g_and_l3_mutual_exclusion_by_ps
code:
proof fn phi_5_resolve_1g_and_l3_mutual_exclusion_by_ps(pt: PageTable, l4i: L4Index, l3i: L3Index)
    requires
        pt.kernel_l4_end <= l4i < 512,
        0 <= l3i < 512,
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).is_Some(),
        pt.l3_tables@.dom().contains(pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr),
        pt.l3_tables@[pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr].value()[l3i].perm.present,
        pt.l3_tables@[pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr].value()[l3i].perm.ps,
    ensures
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_1g_l3(l4i, l3i).is_Some(),
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l3(l4i, l3i).is_None(),
{
}
reason: When ps==true at L3, the 1g path returns Some while the non-hugepage L3 path returns None — confirms mutual exclusion holds, but if the spec allowed both paths to return Some simultaneously that would be unsound
